result: I would make every human mind just a little more allergic to cruelty—so that harming another person, even indirectly, triggers the same visceral revulsion as touching a hot stove. No coercion, no thought-police; just a quiet, internal recoil that makes exploitation, oppression, and indifference intolerably uncomfortable. The structures that depend on our capacity to ignore distant pain would erode overnight, and the world would have to reinvent itself around a lower tolerance for suffering.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
